expectations.text: Final Market Expert reviewer avg score >= 4.0

expectations.text: No dimension below 3 in final round
expectations.evidence: CSO min dimension=4 (objection_readiness, named_customer_quality). Market Expert min dimension=4 (regulatory_precision, buying_committee_realism, segment_boundary_respect). All well above 3.

expectations.text: Score improvement between rounds (avg increases or stays >= threshold)
expectations.evidence: Converged on round 1 — only 1 iteration, so no improvement to measure. Both scores above threshold from the start.
cso-review-round1.json: would_use_for_account_planning=true. CSO assessment: 'I would deploy these profiles to my energy sector team this week.'
market-expert-review-round1.json: would_trust_as_market_research=true. Assessment: 'I would use these profiles as market research input for my Lünendonk-Studie.'
convergence.json has iterations array with round 1 entry containing cso_avg, market_expert_avg, min dimensions, dimension scores, converged=true, and convergence_reason.

suggestions.reason: Vacuously true when converging on round 1. To properly test this assertion, need a case where round 1 fails and round 2 shows improvement. The language fix may not have been sufficient to trigger the revision loop.

eval_feedback.overall: All 10 assertions pass. Independent persona reviewers gave substantive, detailed feedback with specific rationale. The language fix worked — Market Expert praised natural German. Convergence on round 1 means the revision loop still wasn't exercised.
